title: Library preparation from amplicon pool
author: Josh Quick
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bdm7i49n

steps.title: Set up the following reaction for each sample
steps.instruction: Set up the following reaction for each sample: Component Volume DNA amplicons (5ng/ul) 10 µL Nuclease-free water 2.5 µL Ultra II End Prep Reaction Buffer 1.75 µL Ultra II End Prep Enzyme Mix 0.75 µL Total 15 µL

steps.title: Incubate at room temperature for 00:05:00...
steps.instruction: Incubate at room temperature for 00:05:00 Incubate at 65 °C f for 00:05:00 Incubate on ice for 00:01:00

steps.title: Quantify the barcoded amplicon pools using...
steps.instruction: Quantify the barcoded amplicon pools using the Quantus Fluorometer using the ONE dsDNA assay. DNA quantification using the Quantus fluorometer by Josh Quick

steps.title: Set up the following AMII adapter ligation reaction
steps.instruction: Set up the following AMII adapter ligation reaction: Component Volume End-repaired amplicon pools 30 µL Ligation Buffer (LNB) 10 µL Adapter Mix (AMX) 5 µL Quick T4 DNA Ligase 5 µL Total 50 µL There will be some variation in clean-up efficiencies but expect to carry around 80% through a clean-up.

steps.title: Incubate at room temperature for 00:10:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at room temperature for 00:10:00

steps.title: Add 50 µL (1:1) of SPRI...
steps.instruction: Add 50 µL (1:1) of SPRI beads to the sample tube and mix gently by either flicking or pipetting. Vortex SPRI beads thoroughly before use to ensure they are well resuspended, the solution should be a homogenous brown colour.

steps.title: Pulse centrifuge to collect all liquid...
steps.instruction: Pulse centrifuge to collect all liquid at the bottom of the tube.

steps.title: Incubate for 00:05:00 at room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:05:00 at room temperature.

steps.title: Place on magnetic rack and incubate...
steps.instruction: Place on magnetic rack and incubate for 00:02:00 or until the beads have pelleted and the supernatant is completely clear.

steps.title: Carefully remove and discard the supernatant,...
steps.instruction: Carefully remove and discard the supernatant, being careful not to touch the bead pellet.

steps.title: Add 250 µL SFB and resuspend...
steps.instruction: Add 250 µL SFB and resuspend beads completely by pipette mixing. SFB will remove excess adapter without damaging the adapter-protein complexes. Do not use 70% ethanol as in early clean-ups.

steps.title: Pulse centrifuge to collect all liquid...
steps.instruction: Pulse centrifuge to collect all liquid at the bottom of the tube.

steps.title: Remove supernatant and discard
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant and discard.

steps.title: Repeat steps 14-16 to perform a second SFB wash
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 14-16 to perform a second SFB wash.

steps.title: Pulse centrifuge and remove any residual SFB
steps.instruction: Pulse centrifuge and remove any residual SFB. You do not need to allow to air dry with SFB washes.

steps.title: Add 15 µL EB and resuspend beads by pipette mixing
steps.instruction: Add 15 µL EB and resuspend beads by pipette mixing.

steps.title: Incubate at room temperature for 00:02:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at room temperature for 00:02:00 .

steps.title: Place on magnetic rack
steps.instruction: Place on magnetic rack.

steps.title: Transfer final library to a new 1
steps.instruction: Transfer final library to a new 1.5mL Eppendorf tube.

steps.title: Clean-up end-repair reaction using 1x SPRI...
steps.instruction: Clean-up end-repair reaction using 1x SPRI beads Amplicon clean-up using SPRI beads by Josh Quick
